title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 265.492 - Meat advertising to be truthful, accurate
text:
265.492.  Meat advertising to be truthful, accurate. — No person shall advertise for sale, solicit, offer to sell or sell foods by newspapers, handbills, placards, radio, television or other media unless the advertising is truthful and accurate.  Such advertising shall not be misleading or deceiving in respect to grade, quality, quantity or price per pound or piece or in any other manner.
(L. 1985 H.B. 409 & 532 § 28)
